title: The spectrum of consistency models
description: Linearizable, sequential, causal, and eventual consistency trade ordering strength for availability and latency, each with a precise observable definition.
body: Use when specifying or auditing what a read may observe. Linearizable means every operation appears to take effect atomically at a single point between its call and return, giving a real-time total order; sequential drops the real-time requirement; causal preserves only happens-before relationships and allows concurrent operations to be seen in different orders; eventual only promises convergence given no new writes. Do not conflate 'strong' marketing terms with linearizability, and avoid assuming causal+ gives read-your-writes without session guarantees. Actions: pick the weakest model that satisfies the use case and write a checker for it. Validation signals include a model-specific history check (e.g. Knossos for linearizability). Risk boundary: assuming a stronger model than the store provides causes subtle anomalies; verify the documented model against an actual checker.
